title: Yield Curve x Onchain Hashrate x Cross-Spot
notes: This session uses reserve source #26 mempool hashrate in place of the earlier plain-text Blockstream tip concept so the manifest stays JSON-safe and aligned with the generalist source doctrine.

entries.text: The ten-year Treasury yield remains one of the simplest public duration benchmarks, and the FRED JSON path makes it finally usable inside the maintained attestation flow. A current ten-year observation gives this session a long-end rates anchor before the manifest pivots to Bitcoin network production and a cross-venue crypto spot comparison.

Bitcoin hashrate is a cleaner JSON-safe network-production surface than a plain-text tip-height endpoint, which is why it replaces the earlier Blockstream concept here. A current hashrate observation matters because it shows whether mining-side structural commitment remains firm while the broader session is comparing long-end rates and cross-venue BTC spot pricing.

CryptoCompare’s BTC/USD price is a useful sweep input because it gives a lightweight cross-venue reference distinct from Coinbase, Kraken, or Blockchain.info. That makes it a good final leg for a mixed session: the post can ask whether the public cross-spot price view is behaving consistently with firm network production and the still-visible long-end Treasury yield backdrop.
